Technical Assistance

Administration

Executive/Legal 
•• 
Enforcement 
(916) 322-5662 
322·5660 
322·5901 
322-6441 
July 22, 1985 
Richard W. Lofsted 
Recording & Financial Secretary 
Amalgamated Transit Union, Local 265 
843 The Alameda 
San Jose, CA 
95126 
Dear Mr. Lofsted: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-85-l52 
I am in receipt of your request for advice on the propriety 
of your union's political action committee making a contribution 
to the Santa Cruz chapter of the Veterans of Foreign Wars. 
You 
have stated that such a contribution involves "no political 
candidates and np ballot measures" and "has nothing to do with 
elections." 
The Political Reform Act of 1974 (Government Code 
Sections 81000 to 91015) does not regulate this subject except 
to require proper accounting and reporting of any such 
payments. 
However, there are provisions in the Elections Code 
(Sections 12400 to 12407) which govern the proper use of 
campaign funds~ "including funds of political action 
committees." 
For an interpretation of these provisions, you 
should direct your inquiry to the Office of the Attorney 
General. 
In response to your request I am enclosing a copy of our 
current guide for Treasurers of Campaign Committees. 
Should you 
have further questions regarding this letter, please contact me 
at (916) 322-5901.

Dear Ms. Milman, 
1902 Charter Seal 
This is a request 
informal advice on behalf of Amalgamated Transit Union 
Local 265 Political Action Committee (I.D. 841330), of which I am treasurer. 
Executive Board 
our local wishes to provide financial assistance to the 
Santa Cruz chapter 
the Veterans of Foreign Wars, to 
mitigate the 
financial losses 
has suffered while attempting to 
constitutional 
right to freedom 
expression. 
OUr 
has asked Ire 
out 
it is 
proper to use P.A.C. Ironies for this purpose. 
No political 
and no ballot Ireasures are involved, and this has 
nothing to do with elections. Unfortunately, I am unable to retrieve my copy of 
the original PAC guidelines sent to Ire when our committee was 
otherwise 
I would have referred directly to those guidelines. 
In addition to your wr i tten 
response, please send Ire another copy of the guidelines, if it is proper 
your office to do so. 
Thanking you in advance, I am 
RICHARD W. LOFSTED

sections.question: You have stated that such a contribution involves 'no political candidates and np ballot measures' and 'has nothing to do with elections.'
sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act of 1974 (Government Code Sections 81000 to 91015) does not regulate this subject except to require proper accounting and reporting of any such payments. However, there are provisions in the Elections Code (Sections 12400 to 12407) which govern the proper use of campaign funds, 'including funds of political action committees.' For an interpretation of these provisions, you should direct your inquiry to the Office of the Attorney General.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a union political action committee may use its funds to make a financial contribution to the Santa Cruz chapter of the Veterans of Foreign Wars when no political candidates or ballot measures are involved.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC does not regulate this contribution under the Political Reform Act except for accounting and reporting requirements. However, the Elections Code may govern the proper use of campaign funds, and the requestor should consult the Attorney General's office for interpretation of those provisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated July 9, 1985) and the FPPC response letter (dated July 22, 1985). The response letter serves as the primary advice letter. Minor OCR errors noted in original document (e.g., 'np' for 'no', 'Ireasures' for 'measures'), but key content extracted accurately.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have stated that such a contribution involves 'no political candidates and np ballot measures' and 'has nothing to do with elections.'

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act of 1974 (Government Code Sections 81000 to 91015) does not regulate this subject except to require proper accounting and reporting of any such payments. However, there are provisions in the Elections Code (Sections 12400 to 12407) which govern the proper use of campaign funds, 'including funds of political action committees.' For an interpretation of these provisions, you should direct your inquiry to the Office of the Attorney General.

You have stated that such a contribution involves "no political candidates and np ballot measures" and "has nothing to do with elections." The Political Reform Act of 1974 (Government Code Sections 81000 to 91015) does not regulate this subject except to require proper accounting and reporting of any such payments. However, there are provisions in the Elections Code (Sections 12400 to 12407) which govern the proper use of campaign funds~ "including funds of political action committees." For an interpretation of these provisions, you should direct your inquiry to the Office of the Attorney General. embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to a union PAC's inquiry about contributing funds to a Veterans of Foreign Wars chapter, advising that the Political Reform Act requires only proper accounting and reporting, but that the Elections Code may apply and should be interpreted by the Attorney General's office.
